turns.prompt: Design a SEBI compliance monitoring software system that tracks trading activities, reports anomalies, and maintains an immutable audit log.

services.name: API Gateway
services.responsibility: Routes external and internal requests, handles authentication/authorization pre-checks, rate limiting.

services.name: Auth Service
services.responsibility: Manages user authentication, authorization, roles, and permissions.

services.name: Data Ingestion Service
services.responsibility: Connects to external trading platforms, fetches raw trading activity data, performs initial validation, and publishes data to a message queue.

services.name: Trading Data Service
services.responsibility: Stores raw and processed trading activity data in a time-series database and provides APIs for querying this data.

services.name: Rule Management Service
services.responsibility: Allows authorized users to define, update, activate, and deactivate SEBI compliance rules. Stores rule definitions.

services.name: Rule Engine Service
services.responsibility: Subscribes to trading activity data, evaluates it against active compliance rules, and publishes rule violation events.

services.name: Anomaly Detection Service
services.responsibility: Subscribes to trading activity data, applies machine learning models or statistical methods to identify unusual trading patterns, and publishes anomaly detection events.

services.name: Alerting Service
services.responsibility: Subscribes to rule violation and anomaly detection events, generates alerts, manages alert lifecycle (status, acknowledgment), and triggers notifications.

services.name: Notification Service
services.responsibility: Sends alerts and other system notifications via various channels (e.g., email, SMS, internal dashboard).

services.name: Audit Log Service
services.responsibility: Records all significant system events, user actions, and detected anomalies in an immutable ledger database. Provides APIs for querying audit trails.

services.name: Reporting Service
services.responsibility: Generates scheduled and on-demand compliance reports based on trading data, rule violations, and anomalies.

services.name: Dashboard Service
services.responsibility: Provides APIs for the frontend application to display trading activities, compliance status, alerts, and reports.

communication: REST/gRPC for synchronous API calls (e.g., UI to backend, service-to-service queries)
Kafka Event Streaming for asynchronous, high-throughput data ingestion and processing (e.g., Data Ingestion -> Rule Engine -> Anomaly Detection -> Alerting -> Audit Log)
Internal RPC for specific service-to-service communication where direct calls are optimized.
persistence: PostgreSQL: For structured data like user accounts, roles, permissions, rule definitions, alert configurations, report schedules, and metadata.
TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL extension): For high-volume, time-series trading activity data, optimized for time-based queries and retention policies.
Amazon QLDB: For the immutable, cryptographically verifiable audit log of all system actions and detected anomalies.
Redis: For caching frequently accessed data (e.g., active rules, user sessions, dashboard aggregates) and for rate limiting.
deployment: Kubernetes cluster: For container orchestration, deployment, scaling, and management of all microservices.
AWS RDS (PostgreSQL): Managed relational database service for core application data.
AWS QLDB: Managed ledger database service for immutable audit logs.
AWS MSK (Managed Streaming for Kafka) or Confluent Cloud: For the Kafka event streaming platform.
AWS EC2/EKS for TimescaleDB: Self-managed TimescaleDB instances on EC2 or within EKS for time-series data, or a managed TimescaleDB service if available.
AWS ElastiCache (Redis): Managed in-memory data store for caching.
AWS API Gateway: For external API exposure and request routing.
AWS Load Balancers: For distributing traffic across service instances.
scalability: Horizontal auto-scaling for stateless microservices (Data Ingestion, Rule Engine, Anomaly Detection, Alerting, Reporting, Dashboard) based on CPU/memory utilization or custom metrics.
Event-driven architecture with Kafka: Decouples producers and consumers, allowing independent scaling of processing components.
Database read replicas and sharding: For PostgreSQL and TimescaleDB to handle increased read loads and data volumes.
Caching with Redis: Reduces load on primary databases for frequently accessed data.
Asynchronous processing: Offloads heavy computations (e.g., report generation) to background workers to maintain responsiveness.
Stateless service design: Enables easy scaling out of service instances.
